Who is Homelander?
200.a: The most powerful and most dangerous supe — the leader of The Seven, a psychopathic narcissist

200.q: Who is Billy Butcher?
200.a: The leader of The Boys — motivated by his wife's assault by Homelander

200.q: What is Vought International?
200.a: The corporation that creates, manages, and monetises superheroes

200.q: What is Compound V?
200.a: The chemical compound that gives people superpowers — secretly administered to babies

200.q: Who is Starlight?
200.a: Annie January — a genuine hero who joins The Seven and eventually fights against Vought

200.q: Who plays Homelander?
What is The Deep's power?
200.a: He can breathe underwater and communicate with sea creatures

200.q: Who is Hughie Campbell?
200.a: A regular guy who joins The Boys after his girlfriend is accidentally killed by A-Train

200.q: Who is Black Noir?
200.a: A silent, masked member of The Seven — later revealed to be a clone of Homelander

200.q: What does A-Train do to Robin?
200.a: He runs through her at full speed — killing her instantly

400.a: A temporary version of Compound V that gives regular people powers for 24 hours — used by The Boys

400.q: Who is Soldier Boy?
400.a: The original superhero of the golden age — Homelander's genetic father — played by Jensen Ackles

400.q: What is the significance of Homelander and Maeve's relationship?
400.a: They were a couple — Maeve knows his darkest secrets and eventually helps bring him down

400.q: Who is Stan Edgar?
400.a: The cold, calculating CEO of Vought — the only person Homelander fears

400.q: What is The Seven?
400.a: Vought's premier superhero team — analogous to the Justice League — mostly corrupt

400.q: What is Temp V?
400.a: V24 — a short-acting version of Compound V used by Butcher and Hughie to fight supes

400.q: What is the significance of Noir's face?
400.a: He hides horrific burns and scars — in Season 3 it's revealed he is a Homelander clone

400.q: Who is Sister Sage?
400.a: The smartest person on Earth — introduced in Season 4 as Homelander's new strategist

400.q: What is Firecracker?
400.a: A Infowars-style supe influencer who becomes Homelander's devoted follower in Season 4

400.q: What is the Boys' main weapon against supes?
400.a: Compound V antidote and finding leverage — they rarely win in direct combat

What is the satire target of The Boys?
600.a: Corporate superhero culture, American exceptionalism, celebrity worship, and unchecked power

600.q: Who is Stormfront?
600.a: A Nazi from WWII kept young by Compound V — Homelander's manipulative love interest in S2

600.q: What is the significance of Homelander's origin?
600.a: He was raised in a Vought lab with no human contact — explaining his psychological damage and need for approval

600.q: What is the real meaning of Translucent's death?
600.a: Hughie kills him — a nobody killing a powerful supe — establishing that The Boys can fight back

600.q: What is the Supe Terrorist arc?
600.a: Vought manufactures supe villains to keep demand for their heroes high — staged terrorism for profit

600.q: What is Queen Maeve's arc?
600.a: From genuine hero to jaded cynic to redemptive sacrifice — she fakes her death after destroying Soldier Boy

600.q: What does The Boys say about media and perception?
600.a: Vought controls narrative completely — heroes are products, and public perception is more important than reality

600.q: Who is Grace Mallory?
600.a: A former CIA operative who founded The Boys — she has history with Butcher and Soldier Boy

600.q: What is the significance of A-Train's arc?
600.a: A Black superhero who ignores racial justice to maintain his status — eventually forced to confront his complicity

600.q: What is Ryan Butcher's significance?
600.a: Homelander and Becca's son — he has his father's powers. His choices will define the show's endgame
